tagged_story_for_tts: <[silence:3400ms]>Building with atoms. <[silence:1300ms]> Tiny tools, big precision.<[silence:1300ms]>"Nanofabrication" means crafting structures smaller than a virus—by arranging atoms like microscopic Lego blocks.<[silence:700ms]>It’s how we make chips for phones, or coatings that repel water better than a duck’s feathers.<[silence:1300ms]>Imagine carving a mountain range into a grain of sand.<[silence:700ms]>—That’s the scale.<[silence:1300ms]>Tools like electron beams act as chisels, etching patterns onto silicon wafers.<[silence:700ms]>Or scientists grow materials layer by layer, like frost forming on glass—but with atomic control.<[silence:1300ms]>One example:<[silence:700ms]>Your phone’s processor has transistors 10 nanometers wide—1,000x thinner than a hair.<[silence:700ms]>Another:<[silence:700ms]>Nanoparticles in sunscreen block UV light by scattering it like tiny mirrors.<[silence:1300ms]>The trick?<[silence:700ms]>At this size, materials behave strangely.<[silence:700ms]>Gold turns red.<[silence:700ms]>Carbon becomes stronger than steel.<[silence:2000ms]>Three related subjects are quantum dots, self-assembly tech, and molecular machines.<[silence:1200ms]>
clean_story: Building with atoms.  Tiny tools, big precision. "Nanofabrication" means crafting structures smaller than a virus—by arranging atoms like microscopic Lego blocks. It’s how we make chips for phones, or coatings that repel water better than a duck’s feathers. Imagine carving a mountain range into a grain of sand. —That’s the scale. Tools like electron beams act as chisels, etching patterns onto silicon wafers. Or scientists grow materials layer by layer, like frost forming on glass—but with atomic control. One example:Your phone’s processor has transistors 10 nanometers wide—1,000x thinner than a hair. Another:Nanoparticles in sunscreen block UV light by scattering it like tiny mirrors. The trick?At this size, materials behave strangely. Gold turns red. Carbon becomes stronger than steel. Three related subjects are quantum dots, self-assembly tech, and molecular machines.
